title: Outer Heaven Debrief Channel
subtitle: Campbell review / MSX signal archive
summary: A retro radio archive that frames the classic infiltration doctrine: contact discipline, objective focus, and survival through information rather than noise.

transcript.speaker: Campbell
transcript.text: This channel is kept short for a reason. A long transmission gives the enemy time to triangulate the signal.

transcript.speaker: Snake
transcript.text: Then keep the instructions simple.

transcript.speaker: Campbell
transcript.text: Advance quietly, confirm the objective, and only call when the next move is unclear.

transcript.speaker: System
transcript.text: Archive note: early radio doctrine favors minimal support, manual frequencies, and mission memory discipline.

title: Shadow Moses Entry Notes
subtitle: Dock approach / Codec support tape
summary: A mission tape built for the Side Ops prototype. It explains why the first route favors stealth, ration discipline, and Codec calls before direct engagement.

transcript.speaker: Campbell
transcript.text: The dock is exposed, but the enemy is not expecting a full assault. Use that hesitation.

transcript.speaker: Naomi
transcript.text: If you are injured, do not wait until your vitals collapse. Use a ration or call for support.

transcript.speaker: Snake
transcript.text: What about surveillance?

transcript.speaker: Campbell
transcript.text: Cameras and searchlights can break the infiltration before you meet the main guard unit. Chaff buys time, not victory.

transcript.speaker: System
transcript.text: Side Ops note: first alert, low health, keycard recovery, boss phase, and extraction all have Codec triggers.

title: Field Survival: Rations and Silence
subtitle: Naomi / Nastasha joint simulation note
summary: A short systems tape about why survival items, suppressed fire, and non-lethal takedowns matter in the Side Ops scoring model.

transcript.speaker: Naomi
transcript.text: Pain changes decision-making. A wounded infiltrator becomes impatient, and impatience is detectable.

transcript.speaker: Nastasha
transcript.text: A suppressed weapon reduces the signature, but it does not erase the consequence of the shot.

transcript.speaker: Naomi
transcript.text: Non-lethal force preserves mission options. The ranking system should know the difference.

transcript.speaker: System
transcript.text: Gameplay note: shots, kills, neutralizations, damage, rations, and alerts are all tracked separately.

title: Digital Support Protocol
subtitle: Tanker / Big Shell communication behavior
summary: A technical tape explaining the cleaner MGS2-style Codec logic: saved contacts, mission support, and the difference between tactical advice and personal support.

transcript.speaker: Otacon
transcript.text: A digital link is not just clearer. It also creates more ways to verify who is really on the other end.

transcript.speaker: Colonel
transcript.text: Mission support must remain concise. The operative should not be buried under information.

transcript.speaker: Rose
transcript.text: Personal memory and mission memory are different things. Both can affect performance.

transcript.speaker: System
transcript.text: Archive note: this pack uses green monochrome illustrated panels, cleaner signal feedback, and support-role separation.

title: Survival Radio Basics
subtitle: Mission, medical, and field mentor channels
summary: A radio doctrine tape for the MGS3 pack: survival advice, injuries, weapons, food, camouflage, and the separation of specialist contacts.

transcript.speaker: Major Zero
transcript.text: A survival operation is not won by firepower alone. Information is the ration you cannot afford to waste.

transcript.speaker: Para-Medic
transcript.text: Wounds, stamina, and local resources should be treated as mission systems, not flavor text.

transcript.speaker: The Boss
transcript.text: A weapon is only useful when the soldier understands the terrain around it.

transcript.speaker: System
transcript.text: Future module note: add camouflage rating, injuries, food capture, and survival-specific Codec triggers.

title: Battlefield Control Layer
subtitle: Modern war economy / support simulation
summary: A modern-era systems note for adding PMC pressure, battlefield noise, weapon economy, and support overlays without forcing classic frequencies where they do not fit.

transcript.speaker: Otacon
transcript.text: The battlefield is louder now. A support interface has to filter what matters before it reaches the operator.

transcript.speaker: Drebin Channel
transcript.text: Weapons, ammo, and battlefield access can be treated as an economy layer in a future build.

transcript.speaker: System
transcript.text: Simulation note: MGS4 support should feel modern, layered, and less tied to manual frequency dialing.

transcript.speaker: Otacon
transcript.text: Keep the human voice present. Without it, the interface becomes another weapon system.

title: Mother Base Growth File
subtitle: MSF briefing / staff and ops expansion
summary: A Mother Base planning tape that prepares a future management layer: staff, development, side ops, deployment rewards, and briefing files.

transcript.speaker: Kaz
transcript.text: A base is not a menu. It is a living consequence of every mission you choose to run.

transcript.speaker: Big Boss
transcript.text: Then every operation needs a reason to feed back into the base.

transcript.speaker: Kaz
transcript.text: Staff, research, dispatch, and mission intelligence can all unlock new Side Ops layers.

transcript.speaker: System
transcript.text: Future module note: connect tape unlocks to mission rank, found secrets, and boss completions.

title: iDroid Field Intel Deck
subtitle: Open infiltration / cassette-style support
summary: The main MGSV-style tape deck prototype. It favors mission logs, cassette playback, map-style metadata, and replayable intel rather than classic Codec calls.

transcript.speaker: iDroid
transcript.text: Tape selected. Field intelligence index ready.

transcript.speaker: Ocelot
transcript.text: Open terrain changes the meaning of support. Advice has to survive multiple routes.

transcript.speaker: Kaz
transcript.text: Mark the objective, track what changed, and let the operator replay the details on demand.

transcript.speaker: iDroid
transcript.text: Archive note: favorites, listened state, notes, and playback progress are stored locally.

title: Anomaly Contact Report
subtitle: Unstable battlefield signal / quarantine note
summary: A corrupted tape used to test glitch rendering, unstable transcript tags, and anomaly category filtering inside the archive.

transcript.speaker: iDroid
transcript.text: Warning. Audio index partially degraded.

transcript.speaker: Unknown
transcript.text: The signal repeats because the source was never clean.

transcript.speaker: Kaz
transcript.text: Do not treat every strange contact as supernatural. Verify the tactical effect first.

transcript.speaker: System
transcript.text: Archive note: Patriots and anomaly packs can reuse this degraded waveform style.

title: VR Instructor Zero
subtitle: Training grid / prototype challenge briefing
summary: A VR-style tape preparing future short missions: no alert, time attack, CQC challenge, weapon training, and boss rush variants.

transcript.speaker: VR Instructor
transcript.text: Simulation rule one: a clean route is more valuable than a loud victory.

transcript.speaker: System
transcript.text: No Alert, No Kill, Fast Clear, and All Secrets can become independent VR objectives.

transcript.speaker: VR Instructor
transcript.text: Repeat the route until instinct replaces luck.

title: Memory Leak / GW Echo
subtitle: Corrupted support doctrine
summary: A deliberate glitch tape for the Patriots AI pack. It tests broken chronology, repeated orders, unstable speakers, and corrupted UI tone.

transcript.speaker: Colonel
transcript.text: The mission is proceeding normally. The mission is proceeding normally. The mission is—

transcript.speaker: Rose
transcript.text: Do not let the interface decide which memory is yours.

transcript.speaker: System
transcript.text: Chronology mismatch detected. Support personality unstable.

transcript.speaker: Colonel
transcript.text: Continue the simulation. Do not question the shape of the room.

title: Side Ops Design Log 001
subtitle: Developer-facing mission systems note
summary: A practical tape for the app itself: it documents how Codec triggers, mission objectives, rank scoring, custom conversations, and the Tape Archive should interconnect.

transcript.speaker: System
transcript.text: The archive is not separate from the game. It records what the simulation teaches.

transcript.speaker: Codec Engine
transcript.text: Every major mission state can become a replayable conversation, tape, or lore entry.

transcript.speaker: Mission Engine
transcript.text: Ranks should unlock more than badges: briefings, anomaly reports, weapon notes, and future VR variants.

transcript.speaker: System
transcript.text: Implementation note: v0.7 adds favorites, notes, playback state, listened state, filters, and visual waveform generation.

title: VR Route Memory Notes
subtitle: Time Attack recovery pattern
summary: A reward tape unlocked from Dock Sprint. It explains how route memory, objective order, and low-noise movement reduce the need for reactive combat.

transcript.speaker: VR Instructor
transcript.text: A fast route is not the shortest line. It is the route with the fewest corrections.

transcript.speaker: Snake
transcript.text: So the timer punishes hesitation as much as mistakes.

transcript.speaker: VR Instructor
transcript.text: Exactly. Learn where the keycard, camera window, and extraction trigger sit before you sprint.

transcript.speaker: System
transcript.text: VR reward note: this tape unlocks through the Dock Sprint challenge at FOX rank or better.

title: No Alert After-Action
subtitle: Ghost Dock discipline review
summary: A reward tape for clean stealth clears. It frames no-alert play as information control rather than passive waiting.

transcript.speaker: Campbell
transcript.text: A no-alert clear is not about avoiding every risk. It is about choosing the risks the enemy cannot confirm.

transcript.speaker: VR Instructor
transcript.text: Suspicion is recoverable. A full alert is a command failure in this drill.

transcript.speaker: Campbell
transcript.text: Use crouch timing, line-of-sight breaks, and non-lethal force only when the route demands it.

transcript.speaker: System
transcript.text: VR reward note: this tape unlocks through Ghost Dock at FOXHOUND rank or better.

title: Weapon Range Analysis
subtitle: Controlled fire evaluation
summary: A reward tape for the SOCOM Range challenge. It explains why accuracy, shot count, and target discipline are scored separately.

transcript.speaker: Nastasha
transcript.text: A suppressed sidearm gives control, not permission to fire without thought.

transcript.speaker: VR Instructor
transcript.text: The range counts every shot because wasted ammunition creates noise, time loss, and panic.

transcript.speaker: Nastasha
transcript.text: A clean operator knows the difference between a hit and a solution.

transcript.speaker: System
transcript.text: VR reward note: this tape unlocks through SOCOM Range at FOX rank or better.

title: Armored Duel Replay
subtitle: Captain pattern review
summary: A reward tape for the Armored Captain Duel. It breaks down the shield rush, double-shot phase, and why panic fire lowers the rank.

transcript.speaker: Otacon
transcript.text: The armor is not the problem. The problem is that the shield rush makes you give up distance voluntarily.

transcript.speaker: Snake
transcript.text: So bait the rush, step out, then shoot when the recovery starts.

transcript.speaker: Campbell
transcript.text: Correct. Phase two increases pressure, but the answer is still discipline, not panic fire.

transcript.speaker: System
transcript.text: VR reward note: this tape unlocks through Armored Captain Duel at FOX rank or better.

title: Side Ops Design Log 002
subtitle: Tanker Hold Sabotage / mission pack expansion
summary: Design archive for Mission 002. Explains the tanker pack structure, multi-mission selection, and how Codec calls are routed by mission profile.

transcript.speaker: System
transcript.text: Mission pack 002 adds selectable Side Ops profiles and a second side-scroller route.

transcript.speaker: Otacon
transcript.text: The tanker deck uses longer sightlines, more patrol pressure, and a shielded commander boss.

transcript.speaker: System
transcript.text: All major Codec triggers can now resolve through a mission profile instead of hard-coded Dock Infiltration calls.

transcript.speaker: Otacon
transcript.text: Best runs are stored per mission, so each Side Ops route can have its own rank chase.
